column25: Men-only stratum: 232% greater AUC0-inf in c.521 CC vs c.521 TT (95% CI 74-391%, ratio 1.74-4.91, central 3.32). Sex-pooled estimate not in accessible abstract. Mean values not in abstract — unable to pool with delta-method variance.

notes: N(PM)=2 fails N>=4 criterion — excluded from primary, retained as supplementary observation. AUC0-5 not AUC0-inf — different window. EM mean is N-weighted across three c.521 T/T diplotypes (87.1, 90.0, 67.1 ng·h/mL). PM N=2 SD likely unreliable.

extraction_confidence: N(PM)=1 fails N>=4. Reports CL/F not AUC: *1b/*1b 2.01±0.42, *1b/*15 1.11±0.34, *15/*15 0.29 L·kg-1·h-1. Implied AUC ratios (PM/EM = inverse CL): 6.93 (extreme single point). Dose not in abstract.

notes: No PM (CC) group — 30 healthy white males split into *1a/*1a, *1a/*1b or *1b/*1b, *1a/*5 (each N=10). *1a/*5 is c.521 T/C (IM). *1a/*5 AUC 163.0±64.6 vs *1a/*1b or *1b/*1b 74.8±35.6 = 2.18× IM/EM; vs *1a/*1a 114.5±68.6 = 1.42× IM/EM. No PM/EM ratio extractable. Excluded from primary.

No PM (CC) group reported. *1a/*1a 73.2±23.5, *1b/*1b 47.4±19.9, *1a/*15 69.2±23.4, *1b/*15 38.2±15.9 ng·h/mL. *1a/*15 = c.521 T/C heterozygous. *1b allele inversely effects AUC (matches Mwinyi 2004) confounding pure c.521 attribution. Excluded from primary; retained as evidence the CPIC c.521 categorical scaling oversimplifies haplotype structure.

column25: Reports "*15B heterozygous AUC0-12 93% higher" (ratio 1.93), but does not break out CC homozygotes vs TT in the accessible abstract. N=41 enrolled, 6 outliers excluded → 35 analyzed. Per-genotype N not in abstract. Excluded from primary on missing structure.
